title: Debating ideas
canDo: I can state a position and concede a point in a discussion.

chunks.text: I'd argue that it's worth it.
chunks.example: I'd argue that it's worth the cost.

chunks.text: On the whole, it works well.
chunks.example: On the whole, the plan works well.

chunks.text: There's no denying that it's hard.
chunks.example: There's no denying that it's hard.
To some extent, I agree with you.
That said, there are real risks.

grammar.pattern: I'd argue that... / That said,... / To some extent...

reading.passage: People often debate remote work. I'd argue that it's worth it. On the whole, it improves balance, and there's no denying that it saves time. That said, to some extent it can feel lonely, so it really depends on the role.

questions.q: What is the writer's overall view?

script.t: I'd argue that it's worth it.
To some extent, yes, but that said, there are risks.

dictogloss.sentence: On the whole, it works well.

minimalPair.note: the th-sound in 'worth' vs the s-sound in 'worse'.

fluency432.prompt: Argue for or against remote work, then concede a point.

retell.prompt: Summarise the argument about remote work.

task.prompt: Debate a topic: state your view, then concede a point.

writing.model: I'd argue that remote work is positive. On the whole, it saves time. That said, to some extent it can feel isolating.

sentenceCombining.answer: It saves time, but it can feel lonely.
I agree, although there are some risks.

task.prompt: Write a short argument (3-4 sentences): your view plus a concession.

quiz.q: State your position in a debate.
options: I'd argue that it's worth it.

title: Career & priorities
canDo: I can describe my responsibilities and goals at work.

chunks.example: I'm responsible for the budget.

chunks.text: We're aiming to grow this year.
chunks.example: We're aiming to grow this year.
Let's prioritise the deadline.

chunks.text: In the long run, it'll pay off.
chunks.example: In the long run, it'll pay off.

chunks.text: I'd like to highlight one issue.
chunks.example: I'd like to highlight one issue.

grammar.pattern: I'm responsible for... / We're aiming to...

reading.passage: In my role, I'm responsible for the budget. This year we're aiming to grow, so let's prioritise the main deadline. In the long run, careful planning will pay off. I'd like to highlight one risk: hiring too fast.

questions.q: What is the writer responsible for?

script.t: What are we aiming to do this year?
We're aiming to grow, so let's prioritise the deadline.

gap.q: In the long ___ , it'll pay off.

fluency432.prompt: Describe your role and goals at work or study.

retell.prompt: Summarise the speaker's role and goals.

task.prompt: Explain your responsibilities and a goal you're aiming for.

writing.model: I'm responsible for the budget. We're aiming to grow this year, so let's prioritise the deadline.

sentenceCombining.answer: We're aiming to grow, so let's prioritise the deadline.

parts: In the long run it'll pay off.
sentenceCombining.answer: It's hard now, but in the long run it'll pay off.

task.prompt: Write 3 sentences about your role and goals.

quiz.q: State what you're in charge of.
Say what to focus on first.
options: I'd like to highlight one issue.

title: How things are made
canDo: I can describe how something is made and tested using the passive.

chunks.use: Say what something is made from.
chunks.example: It's made of recycled plastic.

chunks.text: It's said that it lasts longer.
chunks.example: It's said that it lasts longer.

chunks.text: The tests are carried out daily.
chunks.example: The tests are carried out daily.

chunks.text: A new version is being developed.
chunks.example: A new version is being developed.
It was introduced last year.

grammar.pattern: is/are + past participle / was introduced / is being developed
grammar.enhance: passive (is made, was introduced, is being developed)

reading.passage: This bottle is made of recycled plastic. It's said that the new design lasts longer. Quality tests are carried out every day, and a lighter version is being developed. The product was introduced last year and sells well.

questions.q: What is the bottle made of?

script.t: Yes, a lighter one is being developed.

fluency432.prompt: Describe how a product is made and tested.

retell.prompt: Summarise how the product is made and tested.

task.prompt: Describe a product: what it's made of and how it's tested.

writing.model: The bottle is made of recycled plastic. Tests are carried out daily, and a lighter version is being developed.

sentenceCombining.answer: It's made of plastic, which is recycled.
It was introduced last year, and it sells well.

task.prompt: Describe how something is made or done, using the passive (3 sentences).

quiz.q: Say what a product is made of.
Say a new version is in progress.
options: A new version is being developed.

title: Writing connectors
canDo: I can link ideas in formal writing with connectors.

chunks.example: However, there are drawbacks.
As a result, sales rose.
In contrast, costs fell.
Furthermore, it saves time.
Nevertheless, we must be careful.

reading.passage: The new policy had clear benefits. As a result, sales rose sharply. Furthermore, customers were happier. However, costs also increased. In contrast, our rivals cut spending. Nevertheless, we believe the policy was the right choice.

script.t: Yes. As a result, sales rose. However, costs increased.

fluency432.prompt: Summarise a change and its results using linkers.

retell.prompt: Summarise the policy and its effects.

task.prompt: Summarise a decision and its effects using two linkers.

writing.model: Sales rose sharply. Furthermore, customers were happier. However, costs increased. Nevertheless, the policy was right.

sentenceCombining.answer: It's cheap; furthermore, it saves time.

task.prompt: Write a short paragraph (4 sentences) using at least two formal linkers.

title: Hypothetical advice
canDo: I can give advice using the second conditional.

chunks.text: If I were you, I'd wait.
chunks.example: If I were you, I'd wait.
I'd rather not risk it.
Suppose it doesn't work?

chunks.text: It would be better to plan first.
chunks.example: It would be better to plan first.

chunks.text: What if we tried another way?
chunks.example: What if we tried another way?

grammar.pattern: If I were you, I'd... / It would be better to...
examples: If I had time, I'd travel.

reading.passage: My friend wants to quit his job tomorrow. If I were you, I'd wait, I told him. I'd rather not risk it without a plan. Suppose the new job falls through? It would be better to plan first. What if we listed the pros and cons together?

questions.q: What does the speaker suggest doing together?

script.t: If I were you, I'd wait.

fluency432.prompt: Give a friend advice about a big decision.

retell.prompt: Summarise the advice given to the friend.

task.prompt: A friend faces a tough choice; give hypothetical advice.

writing.model: If I were you, I'd wait. I'd rather not risk it. It would be better to plan first.

sentenceCombining.answer: If I were you, I'd wait.

parts: It would be better to plan.
sentenceCombining.answer: It's risky, so it would be better to plan.

task.prompt: Give a friend advice on a decision (3-4 sentences) using the second conditional.

options: If I were you, I'd wait.
It would be better to plan first.

title: Recommendations
canDo: I can recommend something and weigh its advantages and downsides.

chunks.example: I strongly recommend it.
It's worth considering.

chunks.text: The main advantage is the price.
chunks.example: The main advantage is the price.
On the downside, it's slow.

chunks.text: All things considered, it's a good deal.
chunks.example: All things considered, it's a good deal.

grammar.pattern: I recommend... / The main advantage is... / On the downside...

reading.passage: I've tested both laptops, and I strongly recommend the lighter one. The main advantage is the long battery. It's worth considering the price too. On the downside, it's a little slow. All things considered, it's a good deal for students.

script.t: I strongly recommend the lighter one.

gist.q: Does B recommend the lighter one?

fluency432.prompt: Recommend a product and give one advantage and one downside.

task.prompt: Recommend something to a friend; give an advantage and a downside.

writing.model: I strongly recommend the lighter laptop. The main advantage is the battery. On the downside, it's slow. All things considered, it's a good deal.

sentenceCombining.answer: It's light, so I recommend it.
It's slow, but it's still good value.

task.prompt: Write a short recommendation (4 sentences) with one advantage and one downside.
